449.reason: The loop condition allows i to equal symList.size(), which leads to IndexOutOfBoundsException when accessing symList.get(i) inside the loop body. It should be `i < symList.size()`.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Error: should be i < symList.size() to prevent index out of bounds; also, after reading the last token, i becomes symList.size(), and accessing symList.get(i) in subsequent conditions will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  The loop condition allows i to equal symList.size(), which leads to IndexOutOfBoundsException when accessing symList.get(i) inside the loop body. It should be `i < symList.size()`.

459.reason: Accessing symList.get(i) without checking if i is within bounds. If i == symList.size(), this throws IndexOutOfBoundsException.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Potential IndexOutOfBounds: when i equals symList.size() at the end of the list; should check i < symList.size() before accessing]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  IndexOutOfBoundsException: When 'char' is the last token or followed by end of list, i equals symList.size(), causing crash. Also lacks bounds check.

408.reason: The logic for rewinding the parser state is incorrect. It decrements i twice relative to the current token position (once in i--, and once in get(i-1)), causing the parser to skip tokens or access invalid indices, which leads to missed error detection or crashes. It should likely be `now = symList.get(i);`.

It should likely be `now = symList.get(i);`.
